original_reasoning: Arabic is sumac and Greek is heliotrope; both are plant names, a clear botanical match at the category level.
Raising pebbles in running naturally yields scattering into many directions.
المحدرج 'rolled/twisted' matches rubbed groats well through the notion of grain rolled off the ear.
The Arabic gloss includes dividing or cutting a figure into parts, which aligns well with a cut-off piece or splinter.
Wide spacious welcome aligns clearly with giving room, offering, and providing.
A wily thief who takes all readily aligns with extreme daring or audacity.
Arabic unruliness and sudden going out aligns well with Greek quarrelling or fighting, especially as an outbreak of contention.
Arabic gives skin shrinking inward, and Greek means shell or husk; both preserve an enclosing outer layer as conceptual core.
